jokes.text: Wow. Exciting, huh? - Finally getting some money. - What do you want, Lindsay?
jokes.setup: Michael sarcastically comments on the excitement of a board meeting about funds
jokes.punchline: His immediate suspicion when Lindsay appears, knowing she wants money
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Michael's weary familiarity with his family's money-grabbing behavior

jokes.text: Mother's neck. - No, that was a good investment. It is easier to look at now, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Michael listing his father's bad investments
jokes.punchline: Lindsay defending their mother's cosmetic surgery as actually worthwhile
jokes.explanation: Classic AD layered joke - dark humor about mother's surgery while revealing family dynamics

jokes.text: I want to have an affair. Now, I'm not sure with who yet, but I'll need lingerie, waxings... These are some real costs, Michael.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asking for money for something worthwhile
jokes.punchline: Casually announcing affair plans with business-like cost breakdown
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Lindsay's shallow priorities and treating infidelity like a business plan

jokes.text: I just feel like using my body. - Well, Lindsay, could you use it over there? I'm trying to grow.
jokes.setup: Lindsay suggestively saying she feels like using her body
jokes.punchline: Tobias completely misses the sexual implication and asks her to move
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment - his complete obliviousness to sexuality delivered naturally

jokes.text: We had sex once in the last year, and he just laid there.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about things with Tobias
jokes.punchline: Lindsay oversharing intimate details about their sex life
jokes.explanation: Classic Lindsay oversharing moment that makes Michael uncomfortable

jokes.text: Well, if I'm gonna have an affair...
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting charity work instead of affair
jokes.punchline: Lindsay agreeing but immediately returning to affair planning
jokes.explanation: Shows Lindsay's single-minded focus and inability to be redirected

jokes.text: I meant 'a matter of'L' and 'D'...' Life and death.
jokes.setup: Michael reading 'matter of land' in his mother's message
jokes.punchline: Lucille's convoluted explanation that it meant 'L and D' for life and death
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Lucille's dramatics and poor communication skills

jokes.text: Buster's jaw clicks when he eats. I think it's what's driving his friends away.
jokes.setup: Life and death emergency involving Buster
jokes.punchline: Trivial jaw clicking that she thinks affects his social life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - melodramatic about minor issues while missing real problems

jokes.text: I seem to remember a certain someone running to Mommy when his algebra teacher made an example of him.
jokes.setup: Michael criticizing Lucille for coddling Buster
jokes.punchline: Lucille revealing Michael did the same thing with his algebra teacher
jokes.explanation: Good comeback showing Michael's hypocrisy, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: The next day, Mr. Vandenbosch was not in school. He was never heard from again.
jokes.setup: Lucille promising to have a chat with the algebra teacher
jokes.punchline: Sinister implication that she had the teacher disappeared
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark AD humor - suggesting murder while maintaining plausible deniability

jokes.text: Pop-Pop? Sorry, miss. Pop-Pop!
jokes.setup: George Michael thinks he spots his grandfather
jokes.punchline: Mistakes an elderly woman for George Sr., highlighting his desperation to see him
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag of George Michael's Pop-Pop sightings

jokes.text: But with hair! Which is probably why he asked for my hair, remember? - Give Pop-Pop your hair! - Give me the hair!
jokes.setup: George Michael explaining why he thinks he saw George Sr.
jokes.punchline: Escalating theory involving hair theft, with Maeby mockingly joining in
jokes.explanation: Good callback that builds the conspiracy theory while showing family dynamics

jokes.text: tunnelled his way through a sewer line, and then stopped to get a candy apple on his way to Mexico!
jokes.setup: George Michael's increasingly elaborate escape theory
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous detail about stopping for candy apple during dramatic prison escape
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfectly absurd detail that breaks the tension

jokes.text: Do you know what it's like to have a sibling who has no source of income except for you? - just one? No. No idea. It sounds wonderful though.
jokes.setup: George Sr. asking if Michael knows what it's like to support a sibling
jokes.punchline: Michael sarcastically noting he supports multiple family members
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Michael's burden and his father's obliviousness

jokes.text: EspeciallyJordan. He's just waiting for me to screw up, so he can blow the whistle on me.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining board member Jordan is watching for mistakes
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally sets up the literal whistle gag that follows
jokes.explanation: Sets up the elaborate whistle-blowing visual gag sequence

jokes.text: Interesting choice of words, Mr. Jordan. He's right. We don't need a whistle-blower. We need a building full of whistle-blowers.
jokes.setup: Jordan threatening to blow the whistle
jokes.punchline: Michael's literal interpretation leading to actual whistles
jokes.explanation: Clever setup for the physical gag with actual whistles being distributed

jokes.text: When you see something wrong, I wa... There you go. I want you to report it.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining the whistle system
jokes.punchline: Board members immediately start blowing whistles, interrupting him
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with immediate consequences for Michael's plan

jokes.text: Let's wait till something actually happens though. Okay, good fun. All right. Hey. Enough! 45, 46, 47... Okay, there's still three whistles left out there.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to control the whistle situation
jokes.punchline: Complete chaos with Michael desperately counting whistles
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Michael's plan completely backfiring

jokes.text: I just need to prove to my wife that I can act like a man. And it's not about sex. I don't just lie there, if that's what you're thinking.
jokes.setup: Tobias asking for money to prove his manhood
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily defensive about sexual performance, echoing Lindsay's earlier complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment - defensive about something no one accused him of

jokes.text: You're in love with your own brother? The one in the army? - No. Your sister's husband. - Michael? Michael! - No, that's your sister's brother. - I'm my sister's brother. - You're in love with me. Me! - I'm in love with Tobias.
jokes.setup: Confusion about who Buster is in love with
jokes.punchline: Increasingly convoluted family relationship explanations
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly constructed confusion sequence - perfect AD style with layered misunderstandings

jokes.text: I can't be expected to live on the $800-a-month army-wife stipend that I'm gonna get. They just don't support the spouses.
jokes.setup: Buster explaining why he needs money before enlisting
jokes.punchline: Treating himself as a military spouse with specific knowledge of stipends
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster - has researched being an army wife despite being male and single

jokes.text: Well, I've always wanted to remake Annie Hall. Except I wouldn't want to get in bed with a green producer like a Sofia Coppola though. Oh, but give me an old pro, like a Robert Redford? Oh, I'd jump into bed with him in a second.
jokes.setup: Buster suggesting a business idea
jokes.punchline: Casually using sexual metaphors about working with producers while missing the implications
jokes.explanation: Classic Buster innocently using sexual language while completely oblivious

jokes.text: And I wouldn't just lie there, Michael Bluth... if that's what you're thinking. - Actually, that time that was what I was thinking.
jokes.setup: Third reference to 'lying there' in sexual context
jokes.punchline: Michael finally admitting he was thinking about it this time
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the running gag with Michael's honest response

jokes.text: Come on! These salon wars have got to stop! - The war, Lindsay. The real war.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's stylist being called up by reserves
jokes.punchline: Lindsay thinking there are literal wars between salons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay moment - completely self-centered worldview where everything relates to her beauty routine

jokes.text: Lindsay was stunned, notjust that she was losing her stylist... but that apparently, there was a war going on.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's confusion about the war
jokes.punchline: Narrator revealing she didn't know about the actual war
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character beat showing Lindsay's complete disconnect from reality

jokes.text: People love to carbo-load. - The bagel place. - There you go.
jokes.setup: Brainstorming what people need for business idea
jokes.punchline: Gob's shallow observation leading to obvious conclusion about bagels
jokes.explanation: Shows the shallow thinking behind their business venture

jokes.text: I guess that's a response.
jokes.setup: Gob and Tobias's awkward silence after Michael mentions trying their proposal
jokes.punchline: Michael's dry acknowledgment of their non-response
jokes.explanation: Good Michael reaction to family's incompetence

jokes.text: One on the lips.
jokes.setup: Physical reunion between uncle and nephew
jokes.punchline: Oscar wanting to kiss Michael on the lips
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Oscar's inappropriate affection

jokes.text: I have lemons, I make lemonade. - That's a very positive attitude. I hate the lemonade business. I hate the grind. You have to grind so many... lemons.
jokes.setup: Michael using common metaphor about making the best of situations
jokes.punchline: Oscar taking it literally and complaining about actual lemon-grinding
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar moment - completely missing metaphors while being oddly specific

jokes.text: You're not a very metaphorical person, are you?
jokes.setup: Oscar's literal interpretation of the lemonade metaphor
jokes.punchline: Michael's understated observation about Oscar's literalness
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that highlights Oscar's cognitive style

jokes.text: That was a song I wrote for David Cassidy; he was gonna sing it on The Mike Douglas Show. But he was too embarrassed to do it in front ofJohn and Yoko.
jokes.setup: Oscar mentioning his song 'All You Need Are Smiles'
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous backstory about David Cassidy being embarrassed in front of famous musicians
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Oscar's inflated sense of his artistic importance

jokes.text: This guy has got nothing in his life. He wrote one song that madeJoan Baez call him 'the shallowest man in the world.'
jokes.setup: George Sr. defending Oscar to Michael
jokes.punchline: Revealing Oscar was called the shallowest man in the world by Joan Baez
jokes.explanation: Excellent character detail with specific, quotable insult from credible source

jokes.text: I mean, they run their... their tanks through there. Occasionally, they stop for a little lemonade, but what's that? That's a buck fifty. You can't make much from that.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Oscar's land is worthless due to military easement
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of tank drivers buying lemonade for $1.50
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist detail delivered matter-of-factly, perfectly AD style

jokes.text: Fellas! Fellas!
jokes.setup: Michael learning the land is worthless
jokes.punchline: Cut to Oscar cheerfully greeting the tank operators on his land
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual confirmation of the absurd detail just described

jokes.text: I'm protesting the war. There's a war going on, you know? - Yeah, I'm the one who told you, and you said it happened ten years ago.
jokes.setup: Lindsay proudly announcing her war protest
jokes.punchline: Maeby pointing out Lindsay's earlier confusion about when the war happened
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Lindsay's continued confusion and Maeby's memory

jokes.text: Actually, I used this one for Michael when he was too cheap to pay for the premium channels. But I added the bit about the stylists.
jokes.setup: Lindsay showing her protest sign
jokes.punchline: Revealing she recycled a sign from protesting cable channels
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay - protests everything with the same sign, showing her superficial activism

jokes.text: Have you seen the agricultural report? Nothing's hotter than coffee, so... the trend is up.
jokes.setup: Gob pitching the coffee shop investment
jokes.punchline: Shallow market research based on temperature rather than actual trends
jokes.explanation: Good Gob moment showing his superficial understanding of business

jokes.text: Gobias Industries. - Go bias. - As in, 'Go buy us some coffee.'
jokes.setup: Revealing their company name
jokes.punchline: Tortured wordplay that barely makes sense and reveals the plan
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly shows their business incompetence

jokes.text: This is the first any of us at Gobias Industries have heard of a land deal.
jokes.setup: Learning about Michael's land investment
jokes.punchline: Tobias speaking as if Gobias Industries is a real company with multiple employees
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tobias taking their fake company seriously

jokes.text: He went around you, around the board... He steamrolled Gobias.
jokes.setup: Gob getting angry about being left out of the land deal
jokes.punchline: Acting as if their non-existent company was deliberately bypassed
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the fake company premise with Gob's indignation

jokes.text: Maybe the worst bluff I've ever seen. Even the members of Gobias agreed on that one.
jokes.setup: Jordan's weak attempt to pressure Michael
jokes.punchline: Narrator treating Gob and Tobias as actual board members of their fake company
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke with narrator playing along with the fake company premise

jokes.text: This is where we're protesting? This isn't right. Where are the cameras? - They're in the free press zone
jokes.setup: Lindsay being placed in a 'free speech zone'
jokes.punchline: Discovering the cameras are in a separate 'free press zone'
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about the absurdity of designated protest zones

jokes.text: Well, we thought you broke out of prison and were on the lam with stolen hair.
jokes.setup: Explaining why they thought George Sr. wasn't in prison
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning the elaborate hair theft theory
jokes.explanation: Good callback that sounds even more absurd when stated simply

jokes.text: Excuse me. Your father's calling from prison. He wants to talk about the land deal you made with his brother. I found this on your desk. Can I keep this?
jokes.setup: Michael denying his father conducts business from prison
jokes.punchline: Secretary immediately announcing prison call about business, plus whistle request
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing that undermines Michael's denial and brings back whistle gag

jokes.text: Thought we were seein' a gay marriage. - We can still hose 'em down.
jokes.setup: Soldiers arriving at Lindsay's protest
jokes.punchline: Disappointed they're not breaking up gay marriage but willing to hose protesters anyway
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about military/police brutality delivered casually

jokes.text: All your water's doing is whetting my appetite for protest. No hair for oil! No hair for oil!
jokes.setup: Lindsay being hosed down for protesting
jokes.punchline: Using phrase 'no hair for oil' instead of 'no blood for oil'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay moment - corrupting serious protest slogan with her beauty obsession

jokes.text: And he should be, you know? He told me to pay off his brother, and I didn't. Instead, I bought his worthless land.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his mistake to his mother
jokes.punchline: Taking blame for not following bad advice that would have been better
jokes.explanation: Good irony showing Michael's misguided self-blame

jokes.text: What's this? What's happening? - It's a hug, Michael. I'm hugging you. - Why?
jokes.setup: Lucille embracing her emotionally vulnerable son
jokes.punchline: Michael completely unfamiliar with physical affection from his mother
jokes.explanation: Perfect family dynamic showing their emotional dysfunction through rare affection

jokes.text: Let Mama take care of it. - No, no. Mom, come on. I don't want you to do anything. Hey! This isn't algebra. I'm a big boy. Mother! Do not make somebody dis... disappear.
jokes.setup: Lucille offering to help Michael with his problems
jokes.punchline: Michael panicking about her making someone disappear like the algebra teacher
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback payoff with Michael realizing the implications of his mother's 'help'

jokes.text: Now there's someone who could have used a good mother. Whore!
jokes.setup: Lucille encountering a random woman while pursuing Oscar
jokes.punchline: Fake sympathy immediately followed by insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment - false compassion immediately undermined by judgment

jokes.text: You still have the neck of a 20-year-old. - And you still have the hair of a lion.
jokes.setup: Oscar and Lucille reuniting romantically
jokes.punchline: Ironic compliments about her surgically altered neck and his wild hair
jokes.explanation: Good character moment playing on their physical attributes and vanity

jokes.text: You know about what I was saying about how people outlive their usefulness? - That was out of line. - I know. But you made a good point.
jokes.setup: Lucille referencing her earlier cruel comment
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging it was wrong but immediately validating the cruel logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille - fake apology that reinforces the original insult

jokes.text: Why have we been spending so much money on whistles?
jokes.setup: Michael's first board meeting with his mother
jokes.punchline: Board member questioning the inexplicable whistle expense
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing the practical consequences of Michael's whistle scheme

jokes.text: I remember a young man who used to say 'But I don't have bad dreams in your bed!' - That was Buster.
jokes.setup: Lucille citing Michael's childhood as example
jokes.punchline: Michael correcting that it was Buster, implying recent behavior
jokes.explanation: Great character beat revealing Buster still sleeps in his mother's bed

jokes.text: I want to be... a cage dancer.
jokes.setup: Lindsay's newfound confidence from protesting
jokes.punchline: Channeling empowerment into becoming a cage dancer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay - misapplying empowerment toward another form of objectification

jokes.text: Smile.
jokes.setup: Oscar courting Lucille with his song
jokes.punchline: Performance of 'All You Need Are Smiles' with single word lyric
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to earlier setup - the 'profound' song is just one word repeated
